heroHeadline: Day 47: Semana Santa Concludes Without CJNG Incidents; O3 Holds; World Cup Security Enters Final Phase (62 Days Out)
heroSubtitle: Day 47 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco. Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026 concludes April 12 after a holiday period free of major cartel incidents — a security accomplishment for the Sheinbaum government. With 62 days until the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening match, Guadalajara's security preparations are entering their critical final phase. O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) — confirmed as CJNG's new leader by El País on April 6 — continues consolidation under a collective council backed by Grupo Élite. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remains at large as the primary rival power center ($5M U.S. bounty). A CJNG-linked meth distribution ring was sentenced in North Carolina on April 9, documenting the cartel's reach into the American Southeast. Operativo Enjambre cumulative total: 67+ officials detained.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, France 24, CGTN, Al Jazeera, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 10, 2026.
